title: 7 Using the Account Activity Function
body_text:
7	Using the Account Activity Function
7.1 	Searching and Viewing Account Information
Transaction history is available on CAS Online for one year.
To use the Account Activity function to search for, view, or download account information:
Log in to CAS Online and select
Account Activity
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select Account Activity
Enter search criteria on the Account Activity page as described below, then select
Submit
. See Figure 2.
Account
. Use the drop-down menu to select an account, or select
All Accounts
to display a consolidated statement.
Start Date
. Select the start date from which to include transactions in the activity request. Use the drop-down menu to specify the month, date, and year. This value defaults to the previous calendar day.
End Date
. Select the end date on which to exclude transactions from the activity request. Use the drop-down menu to specify the month, date, and year. This value defaults to the current day.
Account
Activity
Filter
. Select one of the following options from the drop-down menu:
ГўВҲВ’
Show All Statement Items (Default)
ГўВҲВ’
Show Deposits Only
ГўВҲВ’
Show Withdrawals Only
ГўВҲВ’
Show Late Items Only (displays transactions that were backdated. These items are displayed with an ГўВҖВңLГўВҖВқ in the Account Activity Description field.)
ГўВҲВ’
Search by Amount. Enter an amount in the box to the right of the Account Activity Filter.
ГўВҲВ’
Search by Description. Enter a description in the box to the right of the Account Activity Filter.
Activity Format
. Select one of the following formats from the drop-down menu:
ГўВҲВ’
View Activity with Details (Default). Refer to Section 7.2, Viewing Account Activity, to see sample search results and for procedures on printing the search results.
ГўВҲВ’
View Activity without Details. Refer to Section 7.2, Viewing Account Activity, to see sample search results and for procedures on printing the search results.
ГўВҲВ’
Download to Comma Delimited Text (for use in applications such as Excel). Refer to Section 7.3, Downloading Account Information, for more information.
ГўВҲВ’
Download to PDF. Refer to Section 7.3, Downloading Account Information, for more information.
Figure 2: Account Activity Request
7.2	Viewing Account Activity
Refer to this section if ГўВҖВңView Activity with DetailsГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңView Activity without DetailsГўВҖВқ is selected from the Activity Format drop-down menu on the Account Activity Request (as described in Section 7.1, Searching and Viewing Account Information).
View the following information on the Account Activity page. See Figure 3 and Figure 4.
ГўВҖВ“
Transaction date
ГўВҖВ“
A description of the transaction that includes the 2-character transaction code, a transaction description, and reference number details. See Transaction and Charge Codes, Form 3667, for a list of transaction codes and descriptions. For financial institutions with one settlement account (typically Ontario credit unions), the description also includes the source account details. The source account is the Transit Number associated with the transaction and is identified in the line ГўВҖВҳSrcAcc#nnnnnnnnГўВҖВҷ. The source account is only displayed if it differs from the Transit Number displayed in the Member Number field.
ГўВҖВ“
Transaction amount
ГўВҖВ“
Account balance
Tip
:	Select a column header to re-sort the data.
Note:	A negative symbol (-) specifies a debit transaction.
To print the Account Activity page, select
Print
.
Figure 3: Account Activity Page ГўВҖВ“ One Settlement Account
Figure 4: Account Activity Page ГўВҖВ“ Multiple Settlement Accounts
7.3	Downloading Account Information
Refer to this section if ГўВҖВңDownload to Comma Delimited TextГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңDownload to PDFГўВҖВқ is selected from the Activity Format drop-down menu on the Account Activity Request (as described in Section 7.1, Searching and Viewing Account Information).
If the user selects
Download to PDF
:
Select
Click here to download your statement
when prompted. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Download Account Activity to PDF
View the PDF. See Figure 6 for a sample.
Figure 6: Sample of Data Displayed in PDF
If the user selects
Download to Comma Delimited Text
:
Select
Open
,
Save
,
or
Save as
when prompted.
View the report. See Figure 7 for a sample.
Figure 7: Sample of Data Displayed in Excel (Comma Delimited Text)
END
